applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 and later where the audit subcategory is enabled
name: A Kerberos service ticket was renewed
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4770 records Ticket Granting Service ticket renewal on a domain controller.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event is generated for every TGS renewal and only on domain controllers.

key_fields.explanation: UPN and Kerberos realm of the account requesting ticket renewal; computer accounts typically end with $.
The service account or computer for which the renewed TGS is valid.
Microsoft documents TicketOptions values such as 0x40810010 and encryption types including 0x11 AES128, 0x12 AES256, and 0x17 RC4-HMAC; IpPort 0 indicates localhost.

false_positives: Microsoft states this event is typically informational.
Long-lived normal sessions renew Kerberos service tickets without user-visible activity.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Ticket

detection_notes: 4770 is usually informational, but investigate renewals where TicketEncryptionType=0x17 in an AES-only domain, TicketOptions includes forwarded/renewable patterns such as 0x60810010, or IpAddress renews a privileged account from an unexpected host. Microsoft documents 0x17 as RC4-HMAC and 0x40810010/0x60810010 TicketOptions semantics; combined with unusual 4769/4624 activity this can support T1550.003 Pass the Ticket rather than normal ticket lifetime renewal.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4770
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, TicketOptions, TicketEncryptionType, ServiceName
spl_snippet: index=wineventlog EventCode=4770 | table _time, host, TargetUserName, IpAddress, TicketOptions, TicketEncryptionType, ServiceName
sample_log:
EventID: 4770
TargetUserName: admin@CORP.LOCAL
ServiceName: cifs/fileserver
TicketOptions: 0x60810010
TicketEncryptionType: 0x17
IpAddress: ::ffff:10.0.0.12
IpPort: 49964

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4770
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4770
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4770
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4770
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4770
assumptions: The Windows Security channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 4770

title: Windows Security Event ID 4770
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security

    selection:
        EventID: 4770
    condition: selection

    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.meaning: RC4-HMAC according to the Microsoft event reference.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TicketEncryptionType with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: A documented ticket-options example that must be decoded as a bit field.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TicketOptions with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4770 mean?
faqs.answer: A Kerberos service ticket was renewed on a domain controller.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 4770 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Renewal is normal Kerberos behavior for renewable tickets.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 4770 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Review account, service, encryption type, ticket options, source address, and nearby 4769 and logon activity.

sources.title: 4770: A Kerberos service ticket was renewed
Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Ticket (T1550.003)
